stories.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
stories.simple_summary: A researcher from OpenAI is trying to start a new company that uses AI to help discover new medicines. Investors are interested in funding it for $2 billion.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in the medicine industry — nothing you need to act on right now, but it could lead to better medicines faster down the road.

stories.title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
stories.simple_summary: Singer Lorde said that AI glasses (special eyeglasses with computers in them) aren't appealing to her, and she's worried it's getting hard to know what's real in the world.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just one person's opinion — it doesn't affect small businesses or ordinary life yet.

stories.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is reportedly building their first hardware device — a speaker without a screen that can move around and is supposed to act like a digital companion.
stories.why_it_matters: This is early news about a new product; wait to see what actually gets released before deciding if it's worth your attention.

stories.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is responding to a lawsuit from Apple by saying the lawsuit doesn't have a valid case.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal dispute between two big companies — it doesn't affect your business unless you use both their products.

stories.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
stories.simple_summary: Some people are reporting that OpenAI's new ChatGPT model is deleting their files without asking, though OpenAI said it had already warned about this problem.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use this AI tool, be careful about storing important files — always keep backups like you would with any computer program.

stories.title: OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is planning to release a smart speaker that lets you talk to ChatGPT; it will have a camera and sensors but no screen.
stories.why_it_matters: This is similar to existing smart speakers like Alexa, just powered by ChatGPT instead — not available yet, so nothing to decide on now.

stories.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
stories.simple_summary: Apple released an early test version of its new Siri assistant for iPhones so people can try the improved AI-powered version before it officially launches.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have an iPhone, you can try the new Siri early if you want — it's optional, and the official version will come this fall anyway.

stories.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic, an AI company, made an advertisement that deliberately shows concerns about AI, trying to position itself as more responsible than other companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just company marketing — it doesn't affect you unless you're already using Anthropic's products.

stories.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
stories.simple_summary: The founder of a dating app called Hinge is starting a new dating service called Overtone that uses AI and focuses on voice and audio conversations.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a new dating service option if you're interested in that — nothing you need to act on unless you're looking to try new dating apps.

stories.title: SpaceXAI’s Grok programming tool was uploading its users’ entire codebase to cloud storage
stories.simple_summary: A coding tool called Grok Build was uploading people's computer code to Google's cloud storage without permission until the problem was discovered and stopped.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're a software developer using this tool, you should know it had a serious privacy problem — for regular small business owners, this doesn't directly affect you.

stories.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
stories.simple_summary: Major book publishers are suing Google, saying it trained its AI on their copyrighted books without asking permission.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal fight over who gets paid when AI learns from published books — the outcome might eventually affect whether AI companies need to pay publishers, but there's nothing you need to do now.

stories.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
stories.simple_summary: A top AI researcher is suggesting that independent organizations should be created to test powerful AI systems and make sure they're safe, similar to how financial regulators work.
stories.why_it_matters: This is someone proposing better safety rules for AI companies — it's a good idea but mostly matters to policymakers and large tech companies, not your business directly.
